skill.name: chat-ui
skill.description: React and Next.js chat interface guidance using ui.inference.sh components, including containers, messages, inputs, typing states, and avatars.
skill.summary: Build React and Next.js chat interfaces with reusable shadcn-style components.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell-like patterns, hardcoded URLs, and weak cryptography indicators. The weak cryptography indicators are false positives, while the documented npx commands and remote component registry URL create a real supply chain risk that users should review before running.

medium_findings.title: Remote Component Installation Command
medium_findings.description: The quick start instructs users to run npx shadcn@latest against a remote component registry URL. This is legitimate for a UI component skill, but it executes package tooling and downloads remote content, so users must trust the upstream registry and package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is shown directly in a bash block and references a remote registry URL. It appears intentional and relevant, but it still creates supply chain exposure if executed.

medium_findings.title: Related Skill Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: The related skills section documents npx skills add commands for additional community skills. These commands are not automatically executed, but they expand the trust boundary if users copy them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit installation examples in documentation. There is no evidence of hidden execution, so the risk is user-driven rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: External Documentation and Image URLs
low_findings.description: The skill includes links to ui.inference.sh, cloud.inference.sh, and inference.sh documentation. These are normal marketplace references, with no evidence of credential collection or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links and an image reference. I found no script, beacon, or instruction to transmit local data to these endpoints.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to hash, encryption, or signature code. The cited lines contain metadata, table text, and styling prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API or weak algorithm use at the cited locations. The static pattern appears to match incidental text.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Command with Remote URL
dangerous_patterns.description: A documented npx command installs chat components from a remote URL. This is a known supply chain sensitive pattern because remote package and registry content can change.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and URL are explicit. The risk depends on user execution and upstream integrity, not on hidden behavior in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Skill Installation Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents commands that install related community skills. Users should review those skills separately before running the commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly visible and contextual. They are not executed by the skill, but copied commands can install additional unreviewed content.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent chat interfaces for AI assistants and messaging flows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for reusable React chat components.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of chat containers, message rows, input controls, and typing indicators.
Provides a quick start command for adding components from ui.inference.sh.
Shows React and Next.js composition patterns for simple chat screens.
Explains supported message roles for user, assistant, and system messages.
Links to related skills for agent interfaces, widgets, and Markdown rendering.
limitations: It does not include backend API logic for model calls or message persistence.
It assumes a React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn-style project setup.
It depends on external component registry availability for the quick start command.
It does not audit related skills before recommending their installation commands.

use_cases.title: Prototype an AI Assistant
use_cases.description: Create a usable chat screen with message history, input handling, and loading feedback.

use_cases.title: Standardize Product Messaging
use_cases.description: Use consistent chat primitives across support, onboarding, and assistant workflows.

use_cases.title: Document Chat UI Patterns
use_cases.description: Explain how chat messages, roles, and streaming states should appear in a design system.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Basic Chat Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chat-ui skill to add a basic React chat screen with a message list, text input, and typing indicator.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt Components to My App
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chat-ui skill to adapt the chat container, message, and input components to my existing Next.js layout.

prompt_templates.title: Design Streaming States
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chat-ui skill to plan loading, streaming, empty, disabled, and error states for an AI assistant chat.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Chat Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chat-ui skill to review my chat interface for accessibility, state handling, component boundaries, and visual consistency.

output_examples.input: Build a minimal chat interface for a customer support assistant.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering message display, input submission, disabled loading state, and a typing indicator.

output_examples.input: Improve the empty state and streaming behavior of my AI chat page.
output_examples.output: Specific UI recommendations for first-run content, partial assistant responses, loading feedback, and failed message recovery.

output_examples.input: Explain which chat components I should use for a design system.
output_examples.output: A component breakdown for container layout, message variants, input controls, avatars, and shared styling tokens.

best_practices: Review remote registry content before running installation commands.
Keep message rendering separate from model API and persistence logic.
Test keyboard navigation, focus handling, and disabled states on every chat flow.
anti_patterns: Do not paste installation commands without reviewing the upstream source.
Do not mix streaming transport code directly into presentational message components.
Do not use one message style for user, assistant, system, loading, and error states.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build React and Next.js chat interfaces using reusable chat containers, messages, inputs, and typing indicators.

faq.question: Does it include backend chat logic?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on frontend chat UI structure and leaves model calls, storage, and authentication to the application.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill declares support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill documents npx commands that fetch remote packages or registry content, which creates supply chain risk if users run them.

faq.question: Are the external URLs automatically contacted by the skill?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The URLs appear as documentation links, an image, and commands that require user action.

faq.question: Should users inspect related skills first?
faq.answer: Yes. The related installation commands reference other skills that should be reviewed before installation.
